title: phlegmariurus brachiatus
intro: Phlegmariurus brachiatus, also known as the tree club moss or branch club moss, is a species of evergreen, perennial plant in the family Lycopodiaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe respiratory issues and reduce fever. The health benefits of Phlegmariurus brachiatus include its ability to relieve congestion, coughs, and asthma symptoms, as well as its potential to act as an antiviral agent. Herbal preparations of Phlegmariurus brachiatus can be made from the plant's leaves, stems, or roots, which are typically dried and then used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures.
